topic: What makes motion design read as cool/premium vs merely clear

findings.claim: Linear motion is the #1 amateur tell; elements must accelerate from rest and decelerate into targets. Ease-out for entrances, ease-in-out for moves.
Overshoot-and-settle (bezier control y>1, e.g. cubic-bezier(0.68,-0.6,0.32,1.6)) is the core confident-landing signal; reserve for ONE element per frame.
Duration bands that read as designed: micro-UI <300ms; social transitions 400-600ms; cinematic reveals 800ms-2s; hero moments 1-3s.
Staggered stop points sell composure: primary settles first, secondaries trail 50-100ms, trailing elements overshoot slightly.
Staggered cascades (~60-90ms/item) turn simultaneous pop-in into a reading path.
Secondary motion/follow-through (shadows, accents lagging the primary) is the most overlooked pro differentiator.
Anticipation is three beats (setup, build, action); its LENGTH sets tone - long build = suspense, short snap = surprise. ~6-12 frames at 30fps before a payoff.
The comedic hold: a still beat after the reveal lands (~0.4-0.8s) IS the punctuation - no motion during the hold.
Reveals are architected withholding (telegraph then disclose), distinct from surprise/misdirection.

Reveal primitives by content: scale-from-anchor for icons/badges; mask/wipe for text/photos; trim-path draw-on for lines/diagrams; scale-pullback for the true-scale whoa; fake-morph (crossfade + subtle transform) beats true point-morph for dissimilar silhouettes.
9:16 trades width for DEPTH: 3+ z-layers, foreground anchor, tight blocking.
Two instant designed-look moves: rim/back light separating subject from background, and palette discipline where the single off-palette accent auto-becomes the focal point.
Motion must arrive somewhere: every move resolves to a strong static frame (visual resolution).

technique_library.name: overshootSettle
technique_library.how: bezier y>1, pass 5-10% beyond target, settle 120-200ms
technique_library.when: hero labels/key numbers; one per frame

technique_library.name: trailingSettleOffset
technique_library.how: primary stops first; secondaries +50-100ms; accents lag, lower amplitude

technique_library.name: maskWipeReveal
technique_library.how: matte sweeps across artwork; hard edge for graphics, feathered for soft

technique_library.name: buildOnAssembly
technique_library.how: diagram self-assembles: draw-on lines, scale-in nodes, fade labels, staggered

technique_library.name: morphToChart
technique_library.how: fake-morph crossfade + subtle transform; object dissolves into its data form

technique_library.name: scalePullbackReveal
technique_library.how: tight detail -> pull out to disclose true scale, ease-out landing
technique_library.when: the whoa beat, once per section

technique_library.name: anticipationBeat
technique_library.how: small opposing prep move 6-12 frames before the payoff

technique_library.name: holdPayoff
technique_library.how: 0.4-0.8s still hold after the reveal lands

technique_library.name: squashStretchAccent
technique_library.how: stretch along motion vector, squash on landing, resolve in 100-150ms

technique_library.name: depthLayerRig
technique_library.how: 3+ z-layers with parallax + atmospheric fade (= our Stage3D)

technique_library.name: rimLightSeparation
technique_library.how: bright edge behind subject + slight background darken
technique_library.when: any hero frame at risk of merging

technique_library.name: paletteDiscipline
technique_library.how: limited palette; ONE accent hue used once as the focal signal

technique_library.name: oneReadPerFrame
technique_library.how: single focal element via thirds + silhouette clarity + reserved accent
